title: thlaspi perfoliatum subsp. erraticum
intro: Thlaspi perfoliatum subsp. erraticum, also known as wild arugula or erratic honewort, is a type of flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae). This plant is used in herbal medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Thlaspi perfoliatum subsp. erraticum are said to include the potential to reduce inflammation, boost the immune system, and support digestive health. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall well-being and alleviate various health issues.
